name: Time Complexity
description: Questions on analyzing and expressing time complexity.

questions.question: A single loop over n elements is typically:
questions.explanation: One pass over n elements does a constant amount of work per element, so O(n).

questions.question: Nested loops over n elements each (no early exit) is often:
questions.explanation: For each of n iterations of the outer loop, the inner loop runs n times, so O(n^2).

questions.question: list.insert(0, x) on a list of size n is:
questions.explanation: Inserting at index 0 shifts all elements, so O(n).

questions.question: Looking up an element in a set of n elements is on average:
questions.explanation: Set membership uses hashing and is O(1) average.

questions.question: Sorting n elements with Python's sorted() is:
questions.explanation: Timsort (used by sorted) is O(n log n) in the worst case.
